BulkTriageParams.description:
The scheduled-bulk request (slice 5, audit A-Mc): selector + patch, exactly the inline
bulk-triage shapes. The selector freezes to `target_ids` AT ENQUEUE (never a live selector
in a queue — D38/H8); the drain applies the frozen set.
BulkTriageParams.title: BulkTriageParams

DecisionEditRequest.description: The editable payload fields — anything set becomes the revoke+create pair's change set.
DecisionEditRequest.title: DecisionEditRequest

DecisionPayload.description: What a human decides — identity/lifecycle stamps are minted here, never client-supplied.
DecisionPayload.title: DecisionPayload

ExportParams.description:
The export lens — mirrors the M6 `SearchFilters` facets + the output format. Stored opaquely
as the report doc's `params`; the drain rebuilds a `SearchFilters` from it (a later slice).
ExportParams.title: ExportParams

SlaPolicy.description:
Per-canonical-severity SLA days + KEV override — editable via PUT /settings/sla.
D46 (#274): full-word knob names, HARD rename from crit_days/med_days (dev data disposable
— no aliases; the config doc reseeds on the next write).
SlaPolicy.title: SlaPolicy

UpdateView.description:
Partial update — unset fields keep their stored value. `owner`/`view_id` are not fields
here, so a rename-the-owner write is UNREPRESENTABLE (extra=forbid), not just forbidden.
UpdateView.title: UpdateView

ViewPreset.description:
The stored filter lens — MIRRORS `SearchFilters` one-to-one (drift fails the mirror
test). Closed vocabularies validated here; free-string fields keep the route caps.
ViewPreset.title: ViewPreset

post.description:
Mint the sibling FIRST, then disable the old — the scanner is never token-less.
The sibling inherits the old token's expiry (rotation is not extension, task E m-7).

schema.title: Response Rotate Api V1 Admin Tokens  Token Id  Rotate Post

Response Create User Api V1 Admin Users Post

Response Set Role Api V1 Admin Users  Username  Role Patch
Response Read Audit Log Api V1 Audit Get

get.description:
The prototype's Export CSV (M9d): streams the current audit lens — decorated,
injection-sanitized, constant-memory. Same inline bounds as the findings export: a cheap
pre-count 413s over `JAVV_EXPORT_MAX_ROWS` before any PIT opens; the PIT slot is the
principal's (429 at the cap) and is released when the stream ends.

Rail counts for the audit screen (M9d rework): entity_type/action/actor terms under the
same filters + D28 bound as the walk — the counts describe the current lens, server-side.
With `interval` (day|hour) the response adds `activity`: the audit lens's events-over-time
histogram across the picker window (quiet buckets included as zeros).

get.description:
M5d/FR-8: the risk-accept review surface for accept_final holders — ACTIVE risk-accept
decisions, soonest-expiring first (RULING, #30: creation is already SEC-2-gated, so this is
a review queue over standing acceptances, not a pending-approval workflow).

One repo:tag's committed scan-event history for the DigestSubTimeline — build-change
(digest flips) and gap (per-scanner `scan_order` jumps) markers are derived client-side.

post.description:
First-login `must_change` exit + regular self-service change. Verifies the CURRENT password
(a hijacked cookie alone can't rotate credentials), then revokes every session of the user and
mints a fresh one — a concurrently-stolen session dies with the old password.

schema.title: Response Change Password Auth Password Post
